proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_080 is associated with longer pfs_months (positive Pearson correlation between feature_080 and pfs_months across all 50,000 patients).

analyses.code:
from scipy.stats import pearsonr
pearsonr(df['feature_080'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.702 between feature_080 and pfs_months; mean PFS rises monotonically across feature_080 quartiles (2.50 -> 4.09 -> 5.27 -> 6.96 months). Signed effect is positive; p essentially zero.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of the 3-level ordinal feature_063 are associated with shorter pfs_months (mean PFS decreases monotonically as feature_063 goes 0 -> 1 -> 2).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by feature_063: level 0 = 5.64, level 1 = 4.44, level 2 = 3.29 months. One-way ANOVA F = 2860.8, p ~ 0. Effect from level 0 to level 2 is approximately -2.35 months. Strong monotonic negative gradient.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_056 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_056 = 0 (negative binary main effect).
Patients with feature_042 = 1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_042 = 0 (positive binary main effect).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS feature_056 = 1 vs 0: 3.62 vs 5.16 months; difference = -1.536 months; Welch t-test p ~ 0. Strong, robust negative association.
Mean PFS feature_042 = 1 vs 0: 5.40 vs 4.31 months; difference = +1.098 months; Welch t-test p ~ 0. Strong positive main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_048 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_048 = 0 (negative binary main effect).
Patients with feature_015 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_015 = 0 (negative binary main effect).
Patients with feature_111 = 1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_111 = 0 (positive binary main effect).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS feature_048 = 1 vs 0: difference = -1.050 months; Welch t-test p = 1.5e-178. Strong negative effect.
Mean PFS feature_015 = 1 vs 0: difference = -0.559 months; p = 6e-144. Significant negative effect.
Mean PFS feature_111 = 1 vs 0: difference = +0.568 months; p = 3.7e-143. Significant positive effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_040 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_040 = 0 (negative binary main effect).
Patients with feature_039 = 1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_039 = 0 (positive binary main effect).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS feature_040 = 1 vs 0: difference = -0.459 months; p = 6.1e-70. Significant negative effect.
Mean PFS feature_039 = 1 vs 0: difference = +0.362 months; p = 5.8e-59. Significant positive effect (but see iteration 24 for collinearity with feature_111).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_067 is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher feature_019 is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
Higher feature_101 is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.116; p = 4e-148. Significant negative association of feature_067 with PFS.
Pearson r = +0.100; p = 9e-112. Significant positive association of feature_019 with PFS.
Pearson r = -0.091; p = 3e-93. Significant negative association of feature_101 with PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the five categories of feature_011 (white, black, hispanic, asian, other) - i.e., race is associated with PFS.
Mean pfs_months differs across the four categories of feature_089 (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured) - i.e., insurance type is associated with PFS.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by race: white 4.68, black 4.72, hispanic 4.71, asian 4.74, other 4.76. ANOVA F = 0.85, p = 0.49. NOT significant; range across groups < 0.10 months. Hypothesis not supported.
Mean PFS by insurance: medicare 4.69, private 4.69, medicaid 4.71, uninsured 4.73. ANOVA F = 0.31, p = 0.82. NOT significant; range < 0.05 months. Hypothesis not supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the multi-level ordinal features feature_021, feature_024, feature_016, feature_127, feature_001, feature_073, none shows a clinically meaningful association with pfs_months (null hypothesis sweep).

analyses.result_summary: ANOVA p-values: feature_021 p=0.71, feature_024 p=0.59, feature_016 p=0.38, feature_127 p=0.64, feature_001 p=0.82, feature_073 p=0.27. None significant; max range across levels < 0.16 months. Hypothesis supported (these are noise features).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS regression of pfs_months on feature_080, C(feature_063), feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_015, feature_111, feature_040, feature_039, feature_067, feature_019, and feature_101, the model explains a large fraction of variance (R^2 > 0.5) and feature_080, feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_015, feature_111, feature_040, feature_067, feature_019, feature_101 retain their univariate signs as significant adjusted predictors.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_056 + feature_042 + feature_048 + feature_015 + feature_111 + feature_040 + feature_039 + feature_067 + feature_019 + feature_101', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted R^2 = 0.792. Adjusted coefficients (all p < 0.001 unless noted): feature_080 +0.176/yr, feature_063=1 -1.19, feature_063=2 -2.33, feature_056 -1.55, feature_042 +1.10, feature_048 -0.98, feature_015 -0.58, feature_111 +0.53, feature_040 -0.47, feature_067 -0.077/unit, feature_019 +0.476/unit, feature_101 -0.019/unit. feature_039 dropped to coef +0.014, p=0.32 (no longer significant once feature_111 is in the model). Hypothesis largely supported except feature_039.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_056 on pfs_months is heterogeneous across the three levels of feature_063 (an interaction exists between feature_056 and tumor-grade-like feature_063).

analyses.result_summary: feature_056 main coef -1.60 in feature_063=0; interaction feature_056 x C(feature_063)[T.1] = +0.060 (p=0.014); feature_056 x C(feature_063)[T.2] = +0.137 (p=8e-5). Negative effect of feature_056 is statistically smaller in higher-grade strata, but the absolute magnitude of effect is still nearly identical across grades (-1.60 vs -1.46). Interaction is significant but small. Hypothesis weakly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_042 on pfs_months is heterogeneous across feature_063 levels (interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficients feature_042 x C(feature_063)[T.1] = -0.005 (p=0.83), feature_042 x C(feature_063)[T.2] = -0.043 (p=0.20). Both not significant. The +1.10 month feature_042 effect is uniform across grade. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_056 on pfs_months differs by patient race (feature_011): there is a feature_056 x feature_011 interaction.
After adjusting for clinical covariates, race (feature_011) has no main effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms feature_056 x C(feature_011)[T.black] = -0.06 (p=0.32), [T.hispanic] = -0.05 (p=0.40), [T.other] = -0.10 (p=0.32), [T.white] = -0.10 (p=0.06). All > 0.05. Treatment-like feature_056 effect (-1.5 mo) is consistent across racial groups. Hypothesis NOT supported - no race-specific treatment-effect heterogeneity.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_011) + feature_080 + C(feature_063) + ... + feature_101', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted race coefficients (vs asian reference): black -0.027 (p=0.29), hispanic -0.012 (p=0.62), other -0.011 (p=0.78), white -0.019 (p=0.39). No racial group differs significantly from any other on PFS after adjustment. Hypothesis (null effect) supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_056 on pfs_months differs by insurance category (feature_089): there is a feature_056 x feature_089 interaction.
After adjusting for clinical covariates, insurance category (feature_089) has no main effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms feature_056 x medicare = -0.010 (p=0.79), private = -0.001 (p=0.99), uninsured = -0.135 (p=0.054). None reach p < 0.05. feature_056 effect uniform across insurance strata. Hypothesis NOT supported.

Adjusted insurance coefficients (vs medicaid reference): medicare +0.007 (p=0.66), private +0.001 (p=0.98), uninsured +0.016 (p=0.59). No category differs from any other; range across categories ~ 0.02 months. Hypothesis (null effect) supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_042 on pfs_months differs by patient feature_080 (i.e., there is a feature_042 x feature_080 interaction).
The negative effect of feature_056 on pfs_months differs by patient feature_080 (feature_056 x feature_080 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: feature_042 x feature_080 interaction coef = -0.0018/yr, p = 0.10. Not significant. Effect of feature_042 (+1.10 mo) is uniform across the feature_080 range. Hypothesis NOT supported.

feature_056 x feature_080 interaction coef = -0.0109/yr, p = 6.6e-22. Significant. Negative effect of feature_056 grows by ~0.011 months for each additional unit of feature_080: at feature_080 = 50, effect ~ -1.38; at feature_080 = 80, effect ~ -1.71. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients positive for both feature_042 and feature_111 have substantially longer pfs_months than would be predicted by the sum of the two main effects (super-additive synergy).

analyses.result_summary: Cell means: (0,0) 4.30, (1,0) 4.29, (0,1) 4.31, (1,1) 5.89. Interaction coef = +1.560 months (t = 68.7, p ~ 0). Striking: feature_042 alone has NO benefit, feature_111 alone has NO benefit, but the combination yields ~+1.6 months. Apparent univariate main effects of feature_042 and feature_111 are entirely driven by the interaction. Hypothesis strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_056 and feature_042 act independently on pfs_months (no feature_056 x feature_042 interaction).
feature_056 and feature_048 act independently on pfs_months (no feature_056 x feature_048 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: feature_056 x feature_042 interaction coef = +0.014, p = 0.55. Not significant. feature_056 (-1.55) and feature_042 (+1.10) effects are additive. Hypothesis (null interaction) supported.
feature_056 x feature_048 interaction coef = +0.009, p = 0.81. Not significant. The two negative-direction predictors act additively. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 and feature_048 act independently on pfs_months (no feature_015 x feature_048 interaction).
feature_056 and feature_015 act independently on pfs_months (no feature_056 x feature_015 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: feature_015 x feature_048 interaction coef = +0.007, p = 0.85. Not significant.
feature_056 x feature_015 interaction coef = -0.002, p = 0.95. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_042 on pfs_months is amplified at higher levels of the continuous biomarker feature_019 (feature_042 x feature_019 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: feature_042 x feature_019 interaction coef = -0.034, p = 0.115. Not significant; if anything, the effect is weakly the opposite direction (effect of feature_042 modestly attenuated at higher feature_019). Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_080 and pfs_months is nonlinear: the slope steepens at higher feature_080 (positive quadratic term).

analyses.result_summary: feature_080 linear coef +0.092, quadratic coef +0.000644 (t = 16.9, p ~ 0). Significant positive quadratic term: PFS rises faster at higher feature_080. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no interaction between race (feature_011) and the ordinal grade-like feature_063 on pfs_months (joint test).

analyses.code: anova_lm(reduced, full) where full has C(feature_011)*C(feature_063)
analyses.result_summary: Joint F-test on the 8 race x grade interaction terms: F = 0.36, p = 0.94. No racial heterogeneity in the grade-PFS relationship. Hypothesis (null) supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039 is collinear with feature_111 - once feature_111 is included, feature_039 has no independent effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_039 + feature_111 + feature_042 + ...', data=df).fit(); pd.crosstab(df['feature_111'], df['feature_039'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_039 = 1 in 84.7% of feature_111 = 1 patients vs 15.1% of feature_111 = 0 patients (high overlap). In the joint model, feature_111 coef = +0.535 (p < 1e-200), feature_039 coef = +0.014 (p = 0.35, NOT significant). feature_039's univariate effect is captured entirely by feature_111. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive feature_080-PFS relationship survives adjustment for all other clinical covariates and is not an artifact of confounding (e.g., older patients having less aggressive disease per feature_063).

analyses.code: Adjusted multivariable model coefficient on feature_080
analyses.result_summary: After adjusting for feature_063, feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_015, feature_111, feature_040, feature_067, feature_019, feature_101, feature_080 coefficient is +0.1757 month per unit (t = 343, p ~ 0). Magnitude essentially identical to the unadjusted slope, indicating the relationship is not confounded by the other measured covariates. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the ~80 binary candidate features (treatments / comorbidities / biomarkers) most show no association with pfs_months after multiple-testing correction; only a small subset (feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_015, feature_111, feature_040, feature_039, feature_034) reaches strong significance.

analyses.code: for each binary feature: stats.ttest_ind(...); rank by p; count p<0.05 and p<1e-6
analyses.result_summary: Of 127 candidate features tested univariately, 23 reach p < 0.05. After Bonferroni correction (alpha = 0.05/127 = 4e-4), only 13 features remain significant. Of those, the 8 with |effect| > 0.1 month are: feature_056 (-1.54), feature_042 (+1.10), feature_048 (-1.05), feature_015 (-0.56), feature_111 (+0.57), feature_040 (-0.46), feature_039 (+0.36 [redundant with 111]), feature_034 (+0.11). Hypothesis supported - true clinical signal is concentrated in a small set of features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous feature_053 (mean ~224, std ~80) shows a significant negative association with pfs_months but the magnitude is small relative to feature_080 / feature_063 / feature_056.
Continuous features feature_066, feature_006, feature_065, feature_105 are weakly associated with pfs_months and likely represent low-information laboratory variables.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.0164, p = 2.4e-4. Statistically significant due to N = 50,000 but very small effect size. Hypothesis supported.

feature_066 r=-0.013 (p=3e-3), feature_006 r=-0.013 (p=4e-3), feature_065 r=+0.010 (p=0.025), feature_105 r=-0.009 (p=0.046). All have |r| < 0.02; statistical significance driven by sample size, not clinical relevance. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A parsimonious final multivariable model containing feature_080, C(feature_063), feature_056, feature_048, feature_015, feature_040, feature_067, feature_019, feature_101, and the synergistic feature_042:feature_111 interaction (with feature_042 and feature_111 main effects) explains as much variance in pfs_months as the larger model and confirms the principal effects.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_056 + feature_048 + feature_015 + feature_040 + feature_067 + feature_019 + feature_101 + feature_042*feature_111', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted R^2 = 0.812 (improves over 0.792 by capturing the synergy). Confirmed signed effects (all p < 1e-100): feature_080 +0.176/yr, feature_063=2 -2.33, feature_056 -1.55, feature_048 -0.97, feature_015 -0.58, feature_040 -0.46, feature_067 -0.076, feature_019 +0.476, feature_101 -0.019, feature_042 alone +0.009 (p=0.6, no main effect), feature_111 alone +0.002 (p=0.9, no main effect), feature_042 x feature_111 +1.560 (p ~ 0). Synergy is the dominant treatment-like effect on the positive side. Hypothesis supported.
